**Sara Treleaven Blakely** <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> (born February 27, 1971) is an American businesswoman and philanthropist. She founded Spanx , an intimate apparel company with pants and leggings, in Atlanta, Georgia . <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> In 2024, Blakely created the footwear brand, Sneex. <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup>
In 2012, Blakely was named in *Time")* magazine's " Time 100 " annual list of the 100 most influential people in the world. <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> In 2014, she was listed as the 93rd most powerful woman in the world by *Forbes* . <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup>

title: Founder Of Spanx On Courage, Failure And Fashion | Radio Boston
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Copy the code below to embed the WBUR audio player on your site
Content:
<iframe width="100%" height="124" scrolling="no" frameborder="no" src="https://player.wbur.org/radioboston/2016/12/09/spanx-sara-blakely"></iframe>
Copy embed code
Alison Bruzek
Meghna Chakrabarti
December 09, 2016
Share
Sara Blakely at WBUR. (Robin Lubbock/WBUR)
Sara Blakely was 29 when she founded the shapewear company Spanx. A former door-to-door fax salesperson, she used the $5,000 she'd saved up to create a form of control-top pantyhose that she could wear under her cream-colored slacks.
Now 45, Blakely is the youngest female billionaire in America, according to Forbes. She's also self-made, joining the rarefied air of Oprah Winfrey and Meg Whitman.
Blakely was in Boston to be a keynote speaker at the [Massachusetts Conference For Women](https://www.maconferenceforwomen.org/) , a meeting of 11,000 women in the state. She joined us before her talk to tell us what she's planning next.

title: How Spanx Founder Sara Blakely Went From Selling Fax Machines to Shapewear
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > How to Follow Your Intuition with Sara Blakely of SPANX
Content:
In 1998, Sara Blakely took her frustration as a consumer and set out to revolutionize the undergarment industry with SPANX. Using her skills honed in fax machine sales, Blakely acted as the product’s number one salesperson, landing her shaping underwear in Neiman Marcus and Oprah’s Favorite Things. Blakely has been named one of the 100 Most Influential People in the world by TIME, and was featured on the cover of Forbes Magazine as the youngest self-made female billionaire. Twenty-two years after starting SPANX, Blakely sold a majority stake to Blackstone and became Executive Chairwoman. Blakely shares how SPANX has been profitable since day one, why what you don’t know can actually be your greatest asset, and why it’s important to “bucket” your days in order to spend time on what you’re best at.

title: Sara Blakely on founding SPANX, smarter thinking and taking risks | Virgin
excerpts:
Section Title: Sara Blakely on founding SPANX, smarter thinking and taking risks
Content:
Image from SPANX
by Sara Blakely
15 April 2019
[SPANX](https://www.spanx.com/) Founder Sara Blakely, shares her personal experiences on the power of thinking differently, always asking why, and how the real work begins after the lightbulb moment…
When I first started SPANX almost 20 years ago, I had never taken a business class. Traditional school was hard for me and I was not a good test-taker. I wanted to be a lawyer, but guess what? I bombed the LSAT not once, but twice! I created the first prototype for SPANX not because I had a brilliant business plan and lots of experience, but because I had a pair of white pants, a party to go to and nothing to wear underneath.

title: Sara Blakely: Start Small, Think Big, Scale Fast | Stanford Graduate School of Business
excerpts:
Section Title: Sara Blakely: Start Small, Think Big, Scale Fast
Content:
June 21, 2018
| by
Blaire Briody
!Sarah Blakely speaking at View From The Top. Credit: Kiefer Hickman
Sarah Blakely speaking at View From The Top. Credit: Kiefer Hickman
Almost every entrepreneur in the U.S. knows the story of how Spanx started: Sara Blakely, then 27, was prepping for a party when she realized she didn’t have the right undergarment to wear under white pants. To solve the issue, she pulled out a pair of scissors and cut the feet off her pantyhose. Thus, Spanx was born.
Spanx’s unique shapewear undergarments, plus dozens of other products like pants and leggings, are now available in more than 50 countries. Blakely was named among TIME’s 100 Most Influential People in 2012, and the world’s youngest self-made billionaire by Forbes the same year.
[](https://twitter.com/JennyHutch/statuses/985964099514261505)
